tweets.text:
The new Laguna S 2.1 model by @poolsideai is now free for 2 weeks on Nous Portal.

At 118B total parameters with 8B active, it's quick to run and the most capable model they've released so far.

Try Portal today at https://t.co/3Uj27KjSik

x.name: Cognition (Devin)
x.bio: Makers of Devin, the first AI software engineer. We are an applied AI lab building end-to-end software agents. Join us: https://t.co/4Ss9hvpjRG

tweets.text:
Run Devin Outposts on @e2b to give Devin fast, configurable cloud sandboxes, including access to infrastructure inside your private cloud.

Deploy on E2B:

https://t.co/R0WI2Xu5ki

Run Devin Outposts on @daytonaio to start Linux or Windows sandboxes that start from a snapshot in under 90ms, with repositories, dependencies, and toolchains already in place.

Deploy on Daytona:

https://t.co/cm5Duks3VI

x.name: Deedy Das
x.bio:
Partner at @MenloVentures. Investor: Anthropic, OpenRouter, Modal, Wispr, Inception, Goodfire, PrimeIntellect

prior: Glean, Google Search. Cornell CS.

tweets.text:
This is the first time IMO has been conclusively solved.

Last year, the best models was an unreleased Gemini Deep Think and a experimental OpenAI model which both did 35/42.

This year, 3 models that are open for public use, including a (soon) open weight one, did it for $10-50!

we've repeatedly seen that this is a useful separation of concerns: 

1/ the brain is a managed harness, well-tuned for Claude, auto-upgraded w/ each model release. 

2/ and hands are dispatched on-demand w/ data residency & network isolation. 

video:
https://t.co/A7ohKgQaAe

this is a nice talk from Vercel Ship 2026. Harrison (a core contributor on Claude Managed Agents) covers some of the benefits of splitting the "brain" (agent harness) from the "hands" (@vercel sandboxes inside your VPC): https://t.co/Ep7Hxcoieo

It all takes less time than you might think, too. I kept he introduction as tight as possible. It probably takes about 90 minutes to read.

In about a day, you can do pre- and post-training on a model that'll write coherent responses.

Have you built a language model? You should. It's so much fun to chat with something you made.

Anyone can do it, too. I made an app that teaches the fundamentals and gives you everything you need to build your own: https://t.co/nLjFfpczbV https://t.co/akIMnTzOEG
